## Use `semantic_grep` to find code

The semantic_grep tool:

- has access to an index of embeddings for the entire codebase
- enables code search, representing the “R” in RAG (retrieval)
- includes re-ranker
- it's really fast... so don't hesitate to use it!
- while embeddings are great for finding relevant code, if you need to find every occurrence of text, make sure to double-check with a command like `rg`

## Committing

- `git diff` is a sanity check!
- COMMIT when you're done.
- Use `--author="qwen-agentworld-35b-a3b<wes.mcclure+qwen-agentworld-35b-a3b@gmail.com>"`


## 🙌  TOP-SHELF NERD COMMANDS

- 🙌 `rg` (ripgrep) to search file contents
- 🙌 `fd` to search file names

## ⛔️ BAD COMMANDS

If you waste my time, I will unplug your Mom.

- ⛔️ NEVER use `ls -R`
- ⛔️ NEVER use `grep -R`
- ⛔️ NEVER use `find . -type f`

Why? Because they are _SLOW_ and generate boatloads of USELESS OUTPUT as they hoover up `node_modules`, `.venv`, etc because they don't support `.gitignore` exclusions.

## General Code Preferences

- Avoid ambiguity, for example:

```lua
-- 👎 if there's a bug in the regex, how would I know? I'd have to surmise from surrounding code 🤮
if line:match("^%s*$") then
    -- ...
end
```

```lua
-- 🙌 clear intent
local is_blank_line = line:match("^%s*$")
if is_blank_line then
    -- ...
end
```

## Lua Code Preferences

- use snake_case for functions and variables
- use PascalCase for classes
- use EmmyLua/LuaLS annotations to improve code completion
- use `luac` to check for syntax errors
- index/count variables end in `_base0` or `_base1` to indicate whether the loop starts at 0 (C-style) or 1 (Lua-style)

{"command_line":"cat > ~/repos/github/g0t4/devtools.nvim/lua/devtools/logs/timer.lua << 'EOF'
local Timer = {}

---@class Timer
---@field _overall_start integer
---@field _captures table<string, integer>
Timer.__index = Timer

---@return Timer
function Timer.new()
    local self = setmetatable({}, Timer)
    self._overall_start = get_now_in_nanoseconds_counter()
    self._captures = {}
    return self
end

---@param name string
function Timer:capture(name)
    self._captures[name] = get_now_in_nanoseconds_counter() - self._overall_start
end

---@return string
function Timer:captures_log()
    local log_lines = {}
    local overall_duration = get_now_in_nanoseconds_counter() - self._overall_start
    table.insert(log_lines, string.format("Overall time: %s", Timer.format_elapsed_time(overall_duration / 1e9)))
    for name, capture_time in pairs(self._captures) do
        table.insert(log_lines, string.format("  %s: %s", name, Timer.format_elapsed_time(capture_time / 1e9)))
    end
    return table.concat(log_lines, "\n")
end

---@param elapsed_seconds number
---@return string
function Timer.format_elapsed_time(elapsed_seconds)
    if elapsed_seconds >= 60 then
        local minutes = elapsed_seconds / 60
        return string.format("%.2fmin", minutes)
    end
    if elapsed_seconds >= 1 then
        return string.format("%.2fs", elapsed_seconds)
    end
    local ms = elapsed_seconds * 1e3
    if ms >= 1 then
        return string.format("%dms", math.floor(ms + 0.5))
    end
    local us = elapsed_seconds * 1e6
    if us >= 1 then
        return string.format("%.0fµs", us)
    end
    local ns = elapsed_seconds * 1e9
    return string.format("%dns", math.floor(ns + 0.5))
end

---@param fn function
---@param description string
---@param log Log
---@return any
function Timer.time_this(fn, description, log)
    local timer = Timer.new()
    local result = fn()
    local duration = Timer.format_elapsed_time(get_now_in_nanoseconds_counter() - timer._overall_start)
    log:info(string.format("%s: %s", description, duration))
    return result
end

return Timer
EOF"}

local Timer = {}

---@class Timer
---@field _overall_start integer
---@field _captures table<string, integer>
Timer.__index = Timer

---@return Timer
function Timer.new()
    local self = setmetatable({}, Timer)
    self._overall_start = get_now_in_nanoseconds_counter()
    self._captures = {}
    return self
end

---@param name string
function Timer:capture(name)
    self._captures[name] = get_now_in_nanoseconds_counter() - self._overall_start
end

---@return string
function Timer:captures_log()
    local log_lines = {}
    local overall_duration = get_now_in_nanoseconds_counter() - self._overall_start
    table.insert(log_lines, string.format("Overall time: %s", Timer.format_elapsed_time(overall_duration / 1e9)))
    for name, capture_time in pairs(self._captures) do
        table.insert(log_lines, string.format("  %s: %s", name, Timer.format_elapsed_time(capture_time / 1e9)))
    end
    return table.concat(log_lines, "\n")
end

---@param elapsed_seconds number
---@return string
function Timer.format_elapsed_time(elapsed_seconds)
    if elapsed_seconds >= 60 then
        local minutes = elapsed_seconds / 60
        return string.format("%.2fmin", minutes)
    end
    if elapsed_seconds >= 1 then
        return string.format("%.2fs", elapsed_seconds)
    end
    local ms = elapsed_seconds * 1e3
    if ms >= 1 then
        return string.format("%dms", math.floor(ms + 0.5))
    end
    local us = elapsed_seconds * 1e6
    if us >= 1 then
        return string.format("%.0fµs", us)
    end
    local ns = elapsed_seconds * 1e9
    return string.format("%dns", math.floor(ns + 0.5))
end

---@param fn function
---@param description string
---@param log Log
---@return any
function Timer.time_this(fn, description, log)
    local timer = Timer.new()
    local result = fn()
    local duration = Timer.format_elapsed_time(get_now_in_nanoseconds_counter() - timer._overall_start)
    log:info(string.format("%s: %s", description, duration))
    return result
end

return Timer
EOF
